oneLineSummary: An interactive educational web concept that helps young children learn healthy eating through playful lunchbox building, clear visual grouping, and age-appropriate interaction design.

summary.systemDefinition: A child-focused learning experience designed around nutrition education and simple digital interaction.
coreWorkflowsFeatures: Interactive lunchbox building with colour-based food grouping
Curriculum-aware nutrition learning and visual explanation
Age-appropriate interaction patterns supported by user research
summary.businessOrOperationalImpact: The concept explores how digital learning tools can make nutrition education more engaging and easier for children to understand.
summary.engineeringValue: It highlights accessibility-aware UI thinking, product design structure, and careful alignment between user needs and interaction complexity.

projectNarrative.problem: Nutrition education for young children can be hard to make engaging without overwhelming them with complexity.
projectNarrative.approach: I designed an interactive concept that translates healthy eating guidance into playful actions, simple grouping, and child-friendly visual communication.
projectNarrative.result: The project strengthened my product thinking around audience-aware design, educational interaction, and usability for younger users.

engineeringHighlights: Accessibility-aware interaction design for younger audiences
User research and competitor analysis to shape product direction
Usability thinking tailored to age-appropriate interfaces

reflection.tradeoffs: Designing for children required reducing interaction complexity while still making the learning experience feel playful and rewarding.
lessonsLearned: Audience age changes how navigation, copy, and feedback should be designed.
Accessibility and clarity are deeply connected in educational products.
Research-backed interaction choices are especially important in child-focused design.
futureImprovements: Broader usability validation with target users

display.libraryFocus: product thinking, educational interaction design, and age-appropriate usability
